skill.name: llm-application-dev-langchain-agent
skill.description: Guides production LangChain and LangGraph agent design with async patterns, RAG, observability, deployment, and evaluation practices.
skill.summary: Build production-ready LangChain and LangGraph agents with practical architecture guidance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and medium patterns, but review found they are Markdown examples, inline code names, or prose. No executable scripts, prompt injection attempts, credential exfiltration, or malicious intent were found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Examples Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The static external command findings point to Markdown code fences, inline API names, and checklist text. No shell command execution or executable script was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is inside Markdown guidance and Python examples. There is no Ruby code, shell backtick execution, or tool instruction that runs a command.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Matches
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The high blocker findings do not correspond to cryptographic operations. The cited lines discuss skill metadata, architecture requirements, routing, tool schemas, or descriptions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain no MD5, DES, SHA1, cipher setup, password hashing, or encryption code. The static result appears to match substrings in ordinary prose or identifiers.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network and Reconnaissance Indicators
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The network finding points to an async request handler example, and the reconnaissance finding points to vector search guidance. No external endpoint, system inventory, or exfiltration path was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 197 defines a sample async function named process_request, not an HTTP client call. Line 90 describes vector store hybrid search, not host or account reconnaissance.

content.value_statement: Production agent development often lacks consistent architecture, monitoring, and evaluation patterns. This skill gives structured guidance for LangChain and LangGraph systems.
actual_capabilities: Recommends LangGraph state management patterns for agent workflows.
Outlines RAG setup with embeddings, vector stores, retrieval, and reranking.
Shows async tool, agent invocation, retry, and error handling patterns.
Covers observability with LangSmith, Prometheus, structured logs, and health checks.
Provides a production checklist for deployment, caching, testing, and documentation.
limitations: It is guidance text, not an executable framework or generator.
Some referenced model names and APIs may require current documentation checks.
It references a resources playbook that is not included in the file structure.
It does not provide complete working application source code.

use_cases.title: Plan a Production Agent
use_cases.description: Define the architecture, state graph, memory approach, tool strategy, and deployment checklist for a new agent system.

use_cases.title: Improve RAG Reliability
use_cases.description: Review retrieval, embedding, reranking, caching, and evaluation choices for a LangChain RAG application.

use_cases.title: Prepare Agent Operations
use_cases.description: Add observability, health checks, retries, timeouts, and evaluation planning before production release.

prompt_templates.title: Design My First Agent
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning a new LangChain agent
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a LangChain agent for my use case: [describe task]. Include state, tools, memory, and tests.

prompt_templates.title: Choose RAG Components
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to recommend a RAG pipeline for [domain]. Compare embeddings, vector storage, reranking, and evaluation needs.

prompt_templates.title: Review Agent Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my LangGraph agent design for production readiness. Focus on observability, errors, scaling, and cost.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Multi-Agent Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to redesign this multi-agent workflow: [describe graph]. Propose routing, state boundaries, fallbacks, and evaluation checks.

output_examples.input: Design an agent that answers customer support questions from internal documents.
output_examples.output: A recommended architecture with a LangGraph state, RAG retriever, async support tool, streaming API endpoint, LangSmith tracing, and an evaluation checklist.
A readiness review covering retry behavior, timeout settings, health checks, monitoring metrics, cache strategy, secret handling, and test coverage.
A workflow outline with supervisor routing, specialized agents, shared state, memory strategy, failure handling, and deployment considerations.

best_practices: Confirm current LangChain, LangGraph, and model documentation before implementation.
Start with a small state graph, then add tools, memory, and routing only when needed.
Add tracing, metrics, retries, and evaluations before handling production traffic.
anti_patterns: Do not paste secrets, tokens, or private customer data into prompts or examples.
Do not treat the examples as complete production code without review.
Do not add external tools without timeout, authentication, logging, and failure handling.

faq.question: Does this skill generate a full LangChain application?
faq.answer: No. It provides architecture guidance, examples, and checklists for building one.

faq.question: Does it support LangGraph?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for state graphs, routing, multi-agent orchestration, and checkpointers.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it include security guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends secret handling, error handling, monitoring, and production review practices.

faq.question: Does it make network calls by itself?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill is a Markdown instruction file and does not execute network requests.

faq.question: What should I verify before using it?
faq.answer: Verify current API names, model availability, dependency versions, and deployment requirements.
